hub_subtitle: Inglês para a vida real — falar, entender e ter autonomia no idioma

lesson.menu_title: Life in a Town of 40,000 -- Small Town vs Big City
lesson.menu_desc: Comparing the two lives you have lived -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- Life in a Town of 40,000
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 3 -- Life in a Town of 40,000

listenings.text: People always ask me which one is better, and honestly, it is not that simple. The city is far more convenient. If I wanted anything at eleven at night, I could get it. The public transport was excellent, and there was a much wider range of restaurants and things to do. But it was also the most stressful period of my life. Here, the air is cleaner, the streets are quieter, and my rent is less than half of what I paid before. The hospital is smaller, though, and the nearest big supermarket is forty minutes away. So it is a trade. You give up convenience and you get your time back.

The thing nobody warns you about is how quiet it gets. In the beginning I found it almost frightening. I was used to noise, and suddenly there was nothing, and I could hear my own thoughts, which was not always comfortable. But the cost of living here is so much lower that I work less and I live better. Do I miss the nightlife? Not really. I miss having a bookshop I could walk to. And I miss my friends, of course, although the good ones still visit. It is the healthiest I have ever felt, and that answers the question for me.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom asks Ana which life she prefers. Then Ana says the city was much more convenient. Next, she explains that her town is quieter and cheaper. After that, she admits the facilities are worse and the hospital is smaller. Finally, she says it is the most peaceful place she has ever lived.
